questions.question: Which protocol is responsible for reliable, connection-oriented communication?
questions.explanation: TCP is a connection-oriented protocol that ensures reliable and ordered delivery of data.

questions.question: Which protocol provides connectionless communication suitable for fast transmission?
questions.explanation: UDP is a connectionless protocol designed for situations where speed is more important than reliability.

questions.question: In the client-server paradigm, what is the primary role of the server?
questions.option_a: A. To request services from clients
questions.option_b: B. To offer services and wait for requests from clients
questions.option_c: C. To only store local data
questions.explanation: In the client-server model, the server provides services and passively waits for client requests.

questions.question: What is the main role of a client in the client-server model?
questions.option_a: A. To store all application data
questions.option_c: C. To actively request services from a server
questions.option_d: D. To replace the server during failures
questions.explanation: Clients actively connect to servers to consume services.

questions.question: Which of the following best describes a Peer-to-Peer (P2P) network?
questions.option_a: A. All nodes rely on a single central server
questions.option_b: B. Nodes can act as both service providers and consumers
questions.option_c: C. Only servers communicate with other servers
questions.option_d: D. Communication occurs only through routers
questions.explanation: In P2P networks, nodes can function both as providers and consumers of services.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a Peer-to-Peer application?
questions.explanation: Napster is a classic example of a peer-to-peer file sharing application.

questions.question: Which of the following communication services is commonly provided over networks?
questions.explanation: Networked applications include email, web browsing, instant messaging, and many other communication services.

questions.question: Which property describes UDP communication?
questions.option_d: D. Requires handshake before sending data
questions.explanation: UDP operates without establishing a connection, making it connectionless.

questions.question: Which property describes TCP communication?
questions.explanation: TCP requires a connection to be established before data transmission begins.

questions.question: What is a socket in network programming?
questions.option_b: B. An abstraction used as a communication endpoint
questions.explanation: A socket is a software abstraction that represents an endpoint for network communication.

questions.question: Which two components form the network address of a socket?
questions.option_b: B. IP address and port number
questions.explanation: Each socket is identified by an IP address and a port number.

questions.question: Which type of socket provides reliable two-way communication streams?
questions.explanation: Stream sockets typically use TCP and provide reliable two-way communication.

questions.question: Which socket type is typically associated with UDP?
questions.explanation: Datagram sockets are used for UDP communication.

questions.question: Which layer of the Internet protocol stack includes protocols such as TCP and UDP?
questions.explanation: TCP and UDP are transport layer protocols responsible for process-to-process communication.

questions.question: Which protocol operates at the network layer of the Internet architecture?
questions.explanation: The IP protocol is responsible for addressing and routing at the network layer.

questions.question: What is encapsulation in networking?
questions.option_a: A. Compressing data for faster transmission
questions.option_b: B. Adding protocol headers as data moves down the protocol stack
questions.explanation: Encapsulation occurs when each protocol layer adds its own header to the data.

questions.question: What is the unit of data passed from TCP to IP called?
questions.explanation: The data unit passed from TCP to IP is known as a TCP segment.

questions.question: What is the unit of data transmitted by the IP layer?
questions.explanation: IP sends data in the form of datagrams.

questions.question: What is the unit of data transmitted across the Ethernet link layer?
questions.explanation: Ethernet transmits data as frames.

questions.question: Which statement correctly describes IPv4 communication?
questions.option_c: C. It is connectionless and unreliable
questions.option_d: D. It requires a persistent session
questions.explanation: IPv4 provides connectionless and unreliable datagram delivery.

questions.question: Why is UDP considered unreliable?
questions.option_a: A. It does not support encryption
questions.option_b: B. It lacks sequencing, acknowledgments, and flow control
questions.option_c: C. It cannot transmit large messages
questions.explanation: UDP does not provide reliability mechanisms such as acknowledgments or sequencing.

questions.question: Which feature allows TCP to reorder out-of-order segments?
questions.explanation: TCP uses sequence numbers to ensure proper ordering of data segments.

questions.question: What mechanism does TCP use to confirm successful delivery of data?
questions.explanation: TCP uses acknowledgments (ACKs) to confirm that data has been successfully received.

questions.question: What happens if TCP does not receive an acknowledgment within the round-trip time?
questions.option_d: D. The packet is discarded permanently
questions.explanation: TCP retransmits data if an acknowledgment is not received within the expected time.

questions.question: What is the purpose of TCP flow control?
questions.option_b: B. To prevent a fast sender from overwhelming a slow receiver
questions.option_d: D. To convert datagrams into frames
questions.explanation: TCP flow control ensures the sender does not send more data than the receiver can handle.

questions.question: Which port range is known as well-known ports?
questions.explanation: Ports 0 through 1023 are known as well-known ports controlled by IANA.

questions.question: Which port number is typically used by HTTP web servers?
questions.explanation: Port 80 is commonly used by HTTP web servers.

questions.question: Which ports are typically assigned dynamically to clients?
questions.explanation: Clients are typically assigned ephemeral ports dynamically.

questions.question: What is the port range for dynamic or private ports?
questions.explanation: Dynamic or private ports range from 49152 to 65535.

questions.question: Which server type processes one request at a time before moving to the next?
questions.explanation: An iterative server processes one client request at a time sequentially.

questions.question: Which server type spawns a new process or thread for each client request?
questions.explanation: Concurrent servers create a new process or thread to handle each client request.

questions.question: In TCP communication, what does full-duplex mean?
questions.option_a: A. Only one device can send data at a time
questions.option_b: B. Data can be transmitted only after encryption
questions.option_c: C. Data can be sent and received simultaneously
questions.option_d: D. Communication is limited to one direction
questions.explanation: Full-duplex communication allows simultaneous sending and receiving of data.

questions.question: Why might an application choose UDP instead of TCP?
questions.option_b: B. UDP requires less overhead and provides faster delivery
questions.explanation: UDP is often used where speed is more important than reliability.

questions.question: What happens during demultiplexing at the receiving host?
questions.option_a: A. Data is encrypted before delivery
questions.option_b: B. Headers are removed as data moves up the protocol stack
questions.option_c: C. Data is duplicated across network interfaces
questions.option_d: D. Routers assign new IP addresses
questions.explanation: During demultiplexing, headers added during encapsulation are removed as data moves up the stack.

questions.question: Which of the following best describes a TCP byte stream service?
questions.option_a: A. Data is transferred in fixed-size packets
questions.option_b: B. Data is exchanged as a continuous stream of bytes
questions.option_c: C. Each message boundary is preserved
questions.explanation: TCP provides a continuous byte stream without preserving message boundaries.

questions.question: Which networking architecture layer interacts directly with hardware?
questions.explanation: The link layer interacts with hardware such as network interface cards and device drivers.

questions.question: Which feature helps TCP avoid overwhelming a receiving system?
questions.explanation: TCP uses an advertised window size to control the amount of data sent.

questions.question: Which statement correctly differentiates TCP and UDP?
questions.option_a: A. TCP is connectionless and UDP is connection-oriented
questions.option_b: B. TCP ensures ordered and reliable delivery while UDP does not
questions.option_c: C. UDP guarantees delivery and TCP does not
questions.option_d: D. UDP maintains session state while TCP does not
questions.explanation: TCP ensures reliable and ordered delivery, while UDP provides best-effort delivery without guarantees.
